summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that clients can create tasks with unbounded TTL values, causing task objects to persist indefinitely in memory and enabling denial of service through resource exhaustion.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that creating tasks with extremely large TTL values causes memory accumulation without cleanup, confirming CWE-400/CWE-770 resource exhaustion vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh created multiple tasks with TTL values of 999999999999 milliseconds (31,000 years), and observed that these tasks remain in memory indefinitely without cleanup, demonstrating the DoS vulnerability.
verification_method: The reproduction script creates tasks with unbounded TTL, monitors memory usage, and verifies that task cleanup timers are set to effectively infinite values, confirming the vulnerability.
evidence_produced_by_run: Tasks created with TTL=999999999999 ms (31,000 years) accepted without validation
Task cleanup timers set to extremely large values
Memory accumulation as tasks persist indefinitely
No server-side bounds checking on TTL parameter

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out version 1.25.1, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
